content.problemStatement:
In Chefland, everyone who earns **strictly** more than YYY rupees per year, has to pay a tax to Chef. Chef has allowed a special scheme where you can invest any amount of money and claim exemption for it.

You have earned XXX (X>Y)(X \gt Y)(X>Y) rupees this year. Find the **minimum** amount of money you have to invest so that you don't have to pay taxes this year.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input consisting of two space separated integers XXX and YYY denoting the amount you earned and the amount above which you will have to pay taxes.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer, denoting the minimum amount you need to invest.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y (1 ≤ Y < X ≤ 100).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer – the minimum amount of money that must be invested so that the effective income does not exceed Y.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each pair (X,Y) the answer is X‑Y. 4‑2=2, 8‑7=1, 5‑1=4, 2‑1=1.
10‑9=1, investing 1 rupee leaves effective income 9 which is not taxed.

solution.approach: For each test case the effective income after investing I rupees becomes X‑I. To avoid tax we need X‑I ≤ Y, i.e. I ≥ X‑Y. The smallest such I is exactly X‑Y. So compute X‑Y for every test case and print it.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        X = int(next(it))
        Y = int(next(it))
        out.append(str(X - Y))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
